subcategory: "API Gateway (Dedicated APIG)"
layout: "huaweicloud"
page_title: "HuaweiCloud: huaweicloud_apig_channel_member"
description: |-
  Use this resource to manage a channel member within HuaweiCloud.

data.description: Use this resource to manage a channel member within HuaweiCloud.

children.literal: Use this resource to manage a channel member within HuaweiCloud.
Create APIG channel member by IP address
Create APIG channel member by ECS instance ID
- (Optional, String, ForceNew) Specifies the region where the channel member is located.
If omitted, the provider-level region will be used.
Changing this parameter will create a new resource.
- (Required, String, NonUpdatable) Specifies the ID of the dedicated instance to which the channel
- (Required, String, NonUpdatable) Specifies the ID of the VPC channel.
- (Required, String, NonUpdatable) Specifies the name of the channel member group.
This is used to select a channel member group for easy unified modification of the corresponding server group backend
- (Required, Int, NonUpdatable) Specifies the port number of the channel member.
The valid value is range from
- (Optional, String) Specifies the IP address of the channel member.
-> Required if the type of vpc channel is
- (Optional, String) Specifies the ID of the ECS instance.
Only the English letters, numbers, underscores(
) and hyphens(-) are allowed and the valid value is range
- (Optional, String) Specifies the name of the ECS instance.
Only the Chinese characters, English letters, numbers, underscores(
and the valid value is range from
are required if the type of vpc channel is
- (Optional, Int) Specifies the status of the channel member.
The valid values are as follow:
- (Optional, Bool) Specifies whether this member is the backup member.
When enabled, the corresponding backend service is a backup node and only works when all non-backup nodes fail.
In addition to all arguments above, the following attributes are exported:
- The weight value of the channel member.
- The creation time of the channel member, in RFC3339 format.
- The ID of the member group.
- The health status of the channel member.
Channel members can be imported using their
, the ID of the related dedicated instance, the ID of the related VPC
channel and the name of the related member group, separated by slashes, e.g.
